question: Will there be a space station with artificial gravity by June 2026?
reason: No space station with artificial gravity is operational or planned by June 2026.
evidence: Aegis Station's Gradient One, a proposed artificial gravity testbed, is scheduled for 2035, not June 2026. ([aegisstation.com](https://aegisstation.com/gradientone.html?utm_source=openai))

A Russian patent for a space station with partial artificial gravity was registered in January 2026, indicating early conceptual work without imminent implementation. ([alanews.it](https://www.alanews.it/tecnologia/mosca-registra-il-brevetto-per-una-stazione-spaziale-con-gravita-artificiale/?utm_source=openai))

NASA's International Space Station (ISS) continues to operate without artificial gravity, focusing on other scientific research. ([nasa.gov](https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/spacestation/2026/04/16/station-orbits-higher-as-crew-runs-new-science-experiments/?utm_source=openai))
